# UC-22.14.73 ¬∑ Protection of Information at Rest ‚Äî Storage Encryption Misconfigurations

## Description

Finds newly created volumes or buckets without encryption-at-rest enabled for SC-28 protection of information at rest.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIST 800-53 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes cloud and sourcetypes aws:cloudtrail. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Tune regex to your JSON shape; (2) auto-open CM-3 change if intentional; (3) integrate AWS Config rules for continuous check; (4) evidence for assessor sampling. Indexes required: `index=cloud`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=aws:cloudtrail`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for AWS (Splunkbase 1876).
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `aws:cloudtrail` `CreateVolume` `CreateBucket`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
(1) Tune regex to your JSON shape; (2) auto-open CM-3 change if intentional; (3) integrate AWS Config rules for continuous check; (4) evidence for assessor sampling.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=cloud sourcetype=aws:cloudtrail earliest=-7d eventName IN ("CreateVolume","CreateBucket")
| spath path=responseElements output=responseElements
| regex responseElements="(?i)encrypted.?false|Encryption:?None|null"
| stats count by userName, eventName, aws_account_id
```

Understanding this SPL

**Protection of Information at Rest ‚Äî Storage Encryption Misconfigurations (SC-28)** ‚Äî Finds newly created volumes or buckets without encryption-at-rest enabled for SC-28 protection of information at rest.

Documented **Data sources**: `aws:cloudtrail` `CreateVolume` `CreateBucket`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for AWS (Splunkbase 1876). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: cloud; **sourcetype**: aws:cloudtrail. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=cloud, sourcetype=aws:cloudtrail, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Extracts structured paths (JSON/XML) with `spath`.
‚Ä¢ Filters rows matching a pattern with `regex`.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by userName, eventName, aws_account_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by user, object, result | sort - count```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Protection of Information at Rest ‚Äî Storage Encryption Misconfigurations (SC-28)** ‚Äî Finds newly created volumes or buckets without encryption-at-rest enabled for SC-28 protection of information at rest.

Documented **Data sources**: `aws:cloudtrail` `CreateVolume` `CreateBucket`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for AWS (Splunkbase 1876). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Change.All_Changes` ‚Äî enable acceleration for that model.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Compare a sample of the same events in the source system (domain controller, cloud tenant, or device) the security team would open first. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (non-encrypted creates), Time chart, Single value (count)

## SPL

```spl
index=cloud sourcetype=aws:cloudtrail earliest=-7d eventName IN ("CreateVolume","CreateBucket")
| spath path=responseElements output=responseElements
| regex responseElements="(?i)encrypted.?false|Encryption:?None|null"
| stats count by userName, eventName, aws_account_id
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by user, object, result | sort - count
```

## Visualization

Table (non-encrypted creates), Time chart, Single value (count). Key fields: `aws_account_id`, `eventName`, `userName`.
